content:
The export function that creates a zip file also has a reminder not to exceed 2 gig. If that import doesn't work, you might try this:

  1. click tools>export and then click the 'open profile link'
  2. now exit thunderbird
  3. highlight the entire profile and copy to external media
  4. on win 11 pc, repeat steps 1 and 2
  5. now highlight and delete the contents of the profile and paste the contents copied to external media
  6. start thunderbird on win 11
creator: davidsk

content:
Thanks for getting back to me, problem is on the win7 version, (115.18.0 (32-bit), the only option is to export as a zip file.

I tried copying the profile folder to the win7 pc and importing that but it says nothing to import
creator: kdp99

content:
Then do this:

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation on win 7
  2. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  3. now exit thunderbird
  4. highlight the entire profile and copy to external media
  5. on win 11 pc, repeat steps 1 and 2
  6. now highlight and delete the contents of the profile and paste the contents copied to external media
  7. start thunderbird on win 11
creator: davidsk

content:

On the win11 pc there are several profiles, one is also called 6kqqg7m3.default I replaced these in both local and roaming from the win7 pc, and although it loads OK Thunderbird is still 'blank' with no accounts or emails. Have I messed up ?

content: You might try contacting the developer to see if there is a newer version. Addons often are not compatible immediately after new releases are made available. If you are routinely dependent on addons, you may find the ESR release more compatible.
creator: davidsk

content:
Also, you might consider using the add-on "Add-on Compatibility Check" to help warn you if your current add-ons will work with new versions of Thunderbird. You can then decide if it's OK to upgrade now or not.

https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/addon-compatibility-check/
creator: rednoise
